_meta.description: Curated historical reference events with realized asset moves. The LLM picks the closest analog by mechanism; the frontend shows the analog's actual past moves — NOT a fresh prediction. Update when a major new event occurs and ~60 days of price data is available.
_meta.methodology: Each entry records what actually happened to relevant assets after the event. Sources: FRED, BIS, Bloomberg public data, BIS Annual Reports, IMF GFSR. Moves shown are widely-cited approximations from the event window, not point-precise.

events.name: 2019 Abqaiq Saudi facility attack
events.trigger: Drone strike on Saudi oil processing facility removed ~5% of global supply briefly
events.lessonForToday: Single-facility supply disruptions tend to retrace fast when no broader escalation follows. Watch for evidence of regional spread before assuming sustained move.

events.name: Russia-Ukraine war onset
events.trigger: Russian invasion of Ukraine + Western sanctions on Russia

realizedMoves.BRENT: +30% over 3 weeks, peaked ~$130, retraced to ~$95 by mid-year
realizedMoves.GOLD: +8% over first month, gave back later as DXY rose
realizedMoves.DXY: -0.5% on day 1 (NOT a haven), then +8% over 6 months on rate divergence
realizedMoves.DE10Y: Initially fell on safe-haven bid, then rose +200bp over 12 months on ECB tightening
realizedMoves.USD/RUB: +90% over first 2 weeks, then collapsed back as capital controls imposed

events.lessonForToday: Major-power conflict with sanctions has multi-month effects. Energy importers' currencies face sustained pressure. DXY only rallies later when rate divergence kicks in — early flows can be against the dollar.

events.name: US withdrawal from Iran nuclear deal + sanctions
events.trigger: Trump exits JCPOA, reimposes secondary sanctions on Iran

realizedMoves.TA125: Israel index up ~5% on regional risk premium

events.lessonForToday: Sanctions on oil exporters create sustained supply premium ahead of effective date. Domestic currency of sanctioned country goes into free fall.

events.name: Brexit referendum
events.trigger: UK vote to leave the EU

realizedMoves.FTM: -3.6% on day 1 (smaller drop because GBP fall helped exporters), recovered within 3 weeks
realizedMoves.UK10Y: Fell from 1.4% to 0.6% over 6 months (BoE easing)
realizedMoves.EQUITIES_DM: S&P -3.6% on day 1, recovered within 2 weeks

events.lessonForToday: Trade/political shocks to one country show up most in its currency, less in the equity index when the currency move offsets earnings. Watch the cross-asset divergence.

events.name: COVID-19 pandemic onset
events.trigger: Global pandemic + lockdowns + supply chain freeze

realizedMoves.BRENT: -65% in 6 weeks, briefly negative on WTI futures
realizedMoves.SPX: -34% in 5 weeks, fully recovered in 6 months on Fed/fiscal stimulus
realizedMoves.DXY: +7% in first 2 weeks (safe haven), gave back over the year

events.lessonForToday: Global demand shocks crater commodities even when geopolitics looks contained. Don't conflate news intensity with directional impact.

events.name: US-China tariff escalation
events.trigger: Series of US tariff announcements on Chinese imports

realizedMoves.SOXX: -25% on tariff announcement days, longer-term tracked tariff cycles

events.lessonForToday: Trade-war shocks hit equity indices more than currencies in floating-rate developed countries. Currency moves are bigger in managed-rate regimes (CNY).

events.name: Argentina IMF crisis
events.trigger: Capital flight + IMF program negotiation

realizedMoves.MERV: Local equities -45% in USD terms over 6 months

events.lessonForToday: EM currency crises in countries with thin reserves can run 50%+ over months. Watch reserve adequacy + IMF stance.

events.name: Turkey lira crisis episodes
events.trigger: Erdogan-Fed standoff + heterodox monetary policy

realizedMoves.XU100: Local TRY-denominated index rose in nominal terms (inflation), but -40% in USD terms

events.lessonForToday: EM currency stress doesn't always crash local nominal equity indices when inflation is the driver. Always normalize to USD or real terms for the analyst view.

events.name: European energy crisis (Nord Stream cut-off)
events.trigger: Russian gas supply to Europe disrupted
events.lessonForToday: Energy-import-dependent regions face combined currency + equity + rate stress when supply is structurally compromised. Industrials are the worst-hit equity sector.

events.name: Lehman bankruptcy + GFC
events.trigger: US financial system stress

realizedMoves.GOLD: Sold initially on liquidation, then +50% over 18 months

events.lessonForToday: Systemic financial stress drives sustained DXY strength and EM equity damage. Gold's initial reaction can be a liquidation sell-off before its safe-haven role kicks in.

events.name: Pelosi Taiwan visit + PLA exercises
events.trigger: PLA encirclement exercises post-visit

realizedMoves.TWII: -3% over a week, recovered within 1 month

events.lessonForToday: Brinkmanship without kinetic action typically retraces fast. Markets price the tail not the headline. Persistent encirclement OR kinetic events change the calculation.

events.name: Saudi-Iran diplomatic restoration
events.trigger: China-brokered détente between Riyadh and Tehran
events.lessonForToday: De-escalation news compresses risk premium modestly but doesn't reverse structural supply concerns. Watch what's actually delivered vs announced.

events.name: Israel-Hamas war onset
events.trigger: October 7 attack + Israeli ground operations

realizedMoves.BRENT: +5% on news, retraced within 2 weeks absent Iranian involvement
realizedMoves.USD/ILS: ILS -5% in first month, recovered as IDF mobilization completed

events.lessonForToday: Conflict in non-oil-producer regions has muted commodity reaction unless wider regional involvement appears imminent. Local equity + currency take the hit.

events.name: Draghi 'whatever it takes' speech
events.trigger: ECB commitment to OMT

realizedMoves.DE10Y: Periphery spreads collapsed; Italian 10Y fell from 6.5% to 4% in 3 months

events.lessonForToday: Credible policymaker commitment can reverse a months-long stress cycle in days. Watch what the words actually commit the institution to.

events.name: Saudi-led blockade of Qatar
events.trigger: Saudi/UAE/Bahrain/Egypt sever ties with Qatar
events.lessonForToday: Intra-GCC disputes don't move oil much when supply is unaffected. Watch the supply chain and shipping lanes for the real signal.

events.name: UK 'mini-budget' / LDI crisis
events.trigger: Truss unfunded tax-cut plan
events.lessonForToday: Fiscal credibility shocks in developed markets can trigger gilts/sterling crises in days. The longer-tail risk is pension fund forced selling — watch for liquidity tools in the BoE/Fed response.

events.name: SNB removes EUR/CHF floor
events.trigger: Surprise SNB decision to abandon currency peg

realizedMoves.USD/CHF: CHF +30% intraday before settling at +20% on the day

events.lessonForToday: Pegs end suddenly. When a managed-rate regime is under stress, watch for a sudden break rather than a smooth adjustment.

events.name: Israel-Hezbollah 2006 war (Second Lebanon War)
events.trigger: Hezbollah cross-border raid + Israeli military campaign in Lebanon

realizedMoves.BRENT: +10% over first 2 weeks of conflict, retraced as the war stayed regionally contained

events.lessonForToday: Israel-Hezbollah conflicts without direct Iranian involvement see modest oil reactions (+5-10%) that retrace within weeks. Watch for Iranian/proxy escalation as the regime-change trigger.

events.name: Houthi Red Sea shipping attacks
events.trigger: Houthi missile and drone attacks on commercial shipping in Red Sea / Bab el-Mandeb

realizedMoves.BRENT: +5-8% on each escalation, retraced as Cape route rerouting absorbed disruption
realizedMoves.EQUITIES_EM: muted reaction overall — supply chain impact diffuse

events.lessonForToday: Shipping chokepoint disruptions hit freight rates much harder than oil prices. Watch container indices (FBX, SCFI) more than crude when Suez/Hormuz/Strait flow is the story.

events.name: Hong Kong extradition bill protests
events.trigger: Anti-extradition bill movement + crackdown + Western sanctions discussion

realizedMoves.USD/HKD: no break of peg (tested but held)

events.lessonForToday: HK political stress hits local equities + property hard but the USD peg has held through all major crises since 1983. Mainland China A-shares get spillover effect but smaller magnitude.

events.name: Silicon Valley Bank collapse + regional bank crisis
events.trigger: SVB run + Signature/Silvergate failures + Credit Suisse forced takeover

realizedMoves.US10Y: -50bp in 3 days (flight to safety + rate-cut expectations)
realizedMoves.USD/CHF: Credit Suisse takeover saw brief CHF weakness then recovery

events.lessonForToday: Banking stress drives sharp rate moves before equity reaction. When deposit franchises are in question, watch the front of the curve and credit spreads — equities are downstream signals.

events.name: Sahel region coup wave (Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger)
events.trigger: Military takeovers in Mali (2020, 2021), Burkina Faso (2022), Niger (2023). French/UN withdrawal. Wagner / Russia presence expansion.

realizedMoves.GOLD: Limited direct impact despite Mali being a major producer; supply continued
realizedMoves.Uranium spot (Niger): +50% over 12 months following Niger coup (Niger = ~5% of global supply)

events.lessonForToday: Sahel political instability has remarkably muted commodity impact because mining/extraction continues under whoever's in power. The real signal is uranium for Niger, supply-chain risk for French companies (Orano), and Russian sphere expansion.
